title: Gymnogrammitis dareiformis
intro: Gymnogrammitis dareiformis, also known as Dareiform Fern or Dare's Fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogrammitis dareiformis include various flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant has been used to make teas and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into infusions and salves, to harness its potential health benefits.

uses: Gymnogrammitis dareiformis, a species of fern native to New Zealand, has been used in traditional Māori medicine.
